markdown:
### Nominal Interest Rate


<definition term="Nominal Interest Rate">The **market interest rate before** inflation **is taken into account**.</definition>

### Real Interest Rate


<definition term="Real Interest Rate">The** interest rate **which has been **adjusted for inflation.**</definition>

* It **reflects** the **true** **cost** of **borrowing** or the **real** **return** on **savings**.

### Calculation of Real Interest Rate


1. The **real interest rate** can be calculated using the following formula:
  1. $\text{Real Interest Rate} = \text{Nominal Interest Rate} - \text{Rate of Inflation}$

<callout type="note">The **real interest rate **can be **negative **if the **inflation rate **is **higher **than the **nominal interest rate**.</callout>

<callout type="example">* Imagine that the** annual rate of inflation** is 5%, and the **nominal interest rate is at** 8%. 
* Therefore the **real interest rate will be**:

$$8-5=3\%$$

* If you have 100\$ in the bank, **before inflation** you would have **earned** 8\$, but in reality you **earned** 3\$, **due to inflation**.</callout>

## Importance of Real Interest Rates


The **real interest rate** is important because it **reflects** the **true** **cost** of **borrowing** and the **real** **return** on **savings**.

1. It helps **borrowers** and **savers** make **informed** **decisions** about **loans** and **investments**.
2. It is used by **policymakers** to **evaluate** the **effectiveness** of **monetary** **policy**.
